stories.body: Israel’s latest strikes on the Lebanese city of Tyre drew condemnation in London and Beirut, with UK Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper calling the IDF attacks disproportionate even as she urged both Israel and Iran to preserve their fragile pause in hostilities, while Lebanese President Joseph Aoun told reporters he will refuse to meet Benjamin Netanyahu until a broader war-ending agreement is reached and warned that only negotiations, not military action, can secure northern Israel.
stories.headline: Israeli air strikes pound Tyre as Tehran warns against escalation

stories.body: With the Strait of Hormuz still largely closed, Iran is reviewing a US-backed interim deal that would halt the war and unlock frozen oil revenues even as Donald Trump pressures Israel to rein in strikes on Lebanon; Reuters reports Tehran is taking a stern negotiating line amid sporadic cross-border attacks, while the Guardian notes Netanyahu has paused direct exchanges with Iran but vows to retaliate forcefully if missiles resume and Hezbollah continues firing rockets from southern Lebanon.
stories.headline: US–Iran conflict widens as Washington weighs ceasefire talks and Hormuz blockade

stories.body: Rescue teams on Mindanao are racing to reach isolated communities after Monday’s magnitude 7.8 earthquake crushed buildings, severed roads and power, and left at least 37 dead and nearly 500 injured, with officials warning the toll could climb as hundreds of aftershocks continue to rattle coastal cities, damage hospitals and trigger landslides across the quake-prone southern Philippines.
stories.headline: Hundreds of aftershocks jolt the Philippines amid fears death toll will rise

stories.body: The Senate voted 52-47 to send a $70 billion immigration enforcement boost to the House, giving Donald Trump resources for a nationwide deportation surge even as Republicans split over his $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, with Reuters reporting failed efforts to strip the account and the Associated Press noting the bill also sustains Homeland Security operations such as TSA air marshal deployments through the rest of Trump’s term.
stories.headline: US Senate approves $70 billion ICE funding package despite internal GOP disputes

stories.body: Pope León XIV arrived in Barcelona after a politically charged tour of Madrid, using his first homily in the city’s cathedral—delivered partly in Catalan—to praise Catalonia’s tradition of welcome and exhort residents to become “constructors of unity” beyond polarization, while greeting crowds with bilingual blessings and meeting regional leaders before inaugurating the Sagrada Família’s Torre de Jesús.
stories.headline: León XIV urges unity in Barcelona as Spanish politics roils

stories.body: Court filings reveal Guardia Civil investigators seized around twenty notebooks from former PSOE militant Leire Díez describing alleged efforts to derail corruption cases involving state firms such as SEPI, Correos and Efe, prompting a July summons for party president Cristina Narbona, even as Yolanda Díaz’s Movimiento Sumar grapples with internal upheaval highlighted by the resignation of organization secretary Laura Moreno amid a string of high-profile departures and an impending extraordinary congress.
stories.headline: Leire Díez notebooks deepen PSOE corruption probe over alleged justice sabotage

stories.body: Barcelona officials and the Mossos d'Esquadra have rolled out a multiday security and mobility plan around the papal visit from 9–11 June, imposing traffic bans, parking restrictions and credentialed access across Ciutat Vella, the Raval and routes to the Sagrada Família and Estadi Olímpic to protect events, manage crowds and keep residents informed even as some curbs begin before the pope’s arrival and linger after his departure.
stories.headline: Barcelona faces sweeping mobility lockdown for papal visit
